meta_title: For Students: Claude Code skills setup to Solve slow development speed (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome slow development speed. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Claude Code Setup: Accelerate Your Development Workflow
content.seo_description: Master Claude Code setup to eliminate development bottlenecks. A beginner-friendly guide for students to build faster and ship professional-grade projects.
content.answer_block: To overcome slow development speed, integrate Claude Code into your terminal to automate repetitive boilerplate and architectural scaffolding. By combining Antigravity’s environment orchestration with Claude’s reasoning, you transform your CLI into a high-velocity engineering workstation, allowing you to focus on logic rather than syntax, significantly accelerating your project delivery timeline.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank terminal, the cursor blinking like a metronome counting down your wasted potential. Every hour spent wrestling with environment configuration or tedious boilerplate is an hour stolen from the feature that will define your career. The gap between a student and a senior engineer isn't just knowledge; it's the sheer velocity at which they iterate.

Today, we stop the cycle of slow progress. We are bridging the gap between raw intent and production-ready code by integrating Claude Code with Antigravity. This isn't just about writing code faster; it's about shifting your mindset from a laborer to an architect, ensuring your first projects aren't just finished, but built with professional rigor.

steps.title: Initialize the Development Environment
steps.description: Establish a clean, isolated workspace to ensure your dependencies don't collide. This step prevents the 'it works on my machine' syndrome.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a Node.js development environment with TypeScript and ESLint, ensuring strict typing and optimal path aliases.

steps.title: Map Project Architecture
steps.description: Before writing functions, define the file structure. Claude excels at visualizing directory trees to keep your project scalable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a modular architecture for a SaaS dashboard, separating components, hooks, and services.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create the directory structure for a scalable SaaS dashboard'

steps.title: Automate Boilerplate Generation
steps.description: Stop typing repetitive imports and interfaces. Use Claude to generate foundation code based on your architectural map.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a base API service class with Axios interceptors and error handling logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate the api service class with full error handling'

steps.title: Implement Type-Safe Logic
steps.description: Leverage Claude’s reasoning to bridge the gap between user requirements and complex TypeScript implementation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a custom hook for authentication state management that handles loading, errors, and user sessions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement useAuth hook with full TS interface support'

steps.title: Execute Automated Testing
steps.description: Never ship broken code. Use the CLI to trigger unit tests immediately after feature implementation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write unit tests for the authentication hook covering success, failure, and edge cases.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create jest tests for the auth hook' && npm test

pro_tips: Always provide Claude with your 'source of truth' file, like a README or schema.prisma, to maintain context across multiple sessions.
Use the '--watch' flag in your development server to allow Claude to see real-time error logs and self-correct instantly.
Treat Claude Code prompts like API endpoints: be specific about input formats, expected outputs, and error handling constraints.
Periodically clear the chat history to prevent the model from getting bogged down in stale context when switching features.
Use Antigravity to snapshot your environment configuration once you reach a stable milestone; it acts as a 'save point' for your project progress.
content.next_steps: Now that your environment is optimized, deploy your project to a cloud provider like Vercel or Railway to see your code live in production.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the setup, and the workflow to outpace the competition. Stop building slowly and start building with intent. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
